panels.description: Height of the latest finalized block that marshal knows about
panels.title: Marshal Finalized Height

panels.description: Height of the latest finalized block that the application has processed
panels.title: Marshal Processed Height

panels.description: High-water mark of tracked network buffers per size class (never decremented; capped at max_per_class=8192). A class pinned at its cap is undersized; a class near zero is dead weight.
panels.title: Network Buffers Created

panels.description: Allocations denied because a network size class hit max_per_class — the direct signal the network pool is too small. Any sustained non-zero rate warrants raising the cap for that class.
panels.title: Network Exhaustions/s

panels.description: Requests larger than the pool max (network 1 MiB, storage 8 MiB) bypass pooling entirely and heap-allocate. A steady rate means a hot path is sized past the pool and should either shrink or the pool max should grow.
panels.title: Oversized Requests/s

panels.description: High-water mark of tracked storage buffers per size class (cap 1024 per class). One pool backs every page cache (engine state, engine other, indexer) plus journal write buffers; classes pinned at cap mean the caches could not pre-allocate what they were configured for.
panels.title: Storage Buffers Created

panels.description: Denied storage-pool allocations. Page caches pre-allocate their slots from this shared pool at startup, so exhaustion at runtime means caches plus write buffers oversubscribe the pool configuration.
panels.title: Storage Exhaustions/s

panels.description: Reads served without touching a blob across every contiguous journal (the paged cache itself exports no metrics, so per-journal hit/miss counters are the efficacy proxy; segmented journals are invisible here and the hits-less public-key cache is excluded). Sustained dips are the page cache thrashing.

targets.expr: sum(rate((label_replace({__name__=~".+_cache_hits_total", __name__!~".*public_key.*", deployer_name=~"$Validator", deployer_region=~"$Region"}, "journal", "$1", "__name__", "(.+)"))[$__rate_interval:15s])) / (sum(rate((label_replace({__name__=~".+_cache_hits_total", __name__!~".*public_key.*", deployer_name=~"$Validator", deployer_region=~"$Region"}, "journal", "$1", "__name__", "(.+)"))[$__rate_interval:15s])) + sum(rate((label_replace({__name__=~".+_cache_misses_total", __name__!~".*public_key.*", deployer_name=~"$Validator", deployer_region=~"$Region"}, "journal", "$1", "__name__", "(.+)"))[$__rate_interval:15s])))

panels.title: Journal Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: Which journal is generating blob reads. A single dominant series identifies the cache (or journal) worth resizing first.
panels.title: Journal Cache Misses/s

panels.description: Blob reads that escaped every cache and hit the backend (global miss proxy). Compare against Disk Reads per Second: if this is high while journal hit rates look fine, the misses are coming from uninstrumented (segmented) journals.
panels.title: Backend Reads/s
